March 2026 budgets displayed with 8 categories and amounts

steps.expected: Budget form appears with category dropdown and amount input
steps.observed: Form appeared with category select (showing unbudgeted categories) and amount field
After full page reload, same budget data displayed with identical amounts

steps.expected: Each category shows budgeted, spent, and remaining
steps.observed: Each budget row displays: category name, budgeted amount (e.g. $600.00), Spent amount (e.g. $193.36), and remaining (e.g. $406.64 left)

steps.action: View budget page with multiple categories
steps.expected: Each category has a progress bar proportional to spending
steps.observed: Green progress bars for under-budget categories filling proportionally: ~22% for Bars/Clubs ($45/$200), ~50% for Clothing ($74.50/$150), ~32% for Groceries ($193.36/$600). Orange/yellow bars for high-usage categories like Home Goods (~89%) and Insurance (100%).

steps.action: Add transaction to push Home Goods over $100 budget
steps.observed: After adding $25.50 Home Goods transaction (total $114.75 vs $100 budget), progress bar turned red/coral extending past 100%, with red text '$14.75 over budget'

steps.action: Navigate to February 2026 (no budgets), click 'Copy Previous Month'
steps.expected: Informed that previous month has no data
steps.observed: Toast message: 'No budgets found for the previous month.'

steps.action: Navigate to May 2026 (empty), click 'Copy Previous Month'
steps.observed: Success toast: 'Copied 8 budget(s) from the previous month.' All 8 budget categories appeared with correct amounts matching April ($200, $150, $300, $600, $100, $150, $2000, $50)

steps.action: Click previous/next month arrows on budget page
steps.expected: Month changes and loads correct data
steps.observed: Previous month button navigated from March to February, showing empty state. Multiple navigations worked correctly to reach different months.

steps.action: Navigate to month with no budgets
steps.observed: February 2026 showed: 'No budgets set for February 2026' with message 'Start by setting budget amounts for your spending categories, or copy budgets from a previous month.'

steps.action: View budget page summary section for March 2026
steps.expected: Total budgeted, spent, and remaining shown
steps.observed: Summary cards displayed: Budgeted $3,550.00, Spent $2,646.41, Remaining $903.59. Math verified: $3,550.00 - $2,646.41 = $903.59 ✓

steps.action: Note budget page state (Home Goods: $89.25 spent)
steps.observed: Home Goods showed $89.25/$100, $10.75 left

steps.action: Navigate to transactions, add $25.50 Home Goods expense
steps.observed: Transaction 'TEST-BUD: IKEA Home Goods' created successfully

steps.expected: Home Goods spending reflects new amount
steps.observed: Home Goods now shows $114.75 spent (was $89.25 + $25.50 = $114.75), $14.75 over budget. Total spent also increased.

steps.action: Navigate to budget page for a month with no budgets (December 2025)
steps.observed: Budget page showed piggy bank icon, heading 'No budgets set for December 2025', and helpful text 'Start by setting budget amounts for your spending categories, or copy budgets from a previous month.' with Set Budget and Copy Previous Month buttons available as CTAs

steps.action: Create $100 Groceries transaction 'TEST-BUD: Costco Split Purchase'
steps.observed: Transaction created with $100 in Groceries category
Split form opened with two rows, categories selected (Groceries/$60 + Eating Out/$40), Save Splits button enabled and clicked successfully
API returned: split 1: Groceries $60 (6000 cents), split 2: Eating Out $40 (4000 cents) — correct

steps.expected: Budget reflects split amounts, not full $100 to one category
steps.observed: Budget page shows Groceries and Eating Out with updated amounts. The split correctly distributes spending across categories rather than assigning full amount to one.

steps.action: Create Groceries budget for January and February 2026 ($500 each)
steps.observed: Budgets created via API (IDs 25, 26)
Transaction 'TEST-BUD: Jan 31 Grocery' created with $75, posted_at 2026-01-31
Transaction 'TEST-BUD: Feb 1 Grocery' created with $50, posted_at 2026-02-01
January 2026 budget page: Groceries Spent $75.00, $425.00 left — only the Jan 31 transaction counted
February 2026 budget page: Groceries Spent $50.00, $450.00 left — only the Feb 1 transaction counted

frictions.description: Categories are managed on the /settings page, not a dedicated /categories route. The settings page combines categories and bank connections. Navigation to /categories returns 404.
frictions.resolution: Found categories under Settings page by exploring navigation

frictions.description: The transaction list split/edit/delete buttons are not always visible in agent-browser interactive snapshots when the element is out of viewport. Required using JS eval to find and click split buttons.
frictions.resolution: Used JavaScript eval to scrollIntoView and click split button when it was outside viewport

frictions.description: Budget category dropdown and transaction filter category list are scrollable but custom categories (like 'TEST-BUD: Utilities') appear below the fold and aren't captured in standard snapshots. DOM queries needed to confirm presence.
frictions.resolution: Used agent-browser eval to query all select options / filter buttons in the DOM

summary: Tested 16 assertions: all 16 passed. Categories (3/3 pass): predefined categories display correctly, custom categories can be created with duplicate rejection, and categories have consistent visual identification across all views. Budgets (7/7 pass): budget creation, persistence, vs-actual display, progress bars, over-budget indicators, copy-from-previous, month navigation, and summary totals all work correctly. Cross-feature (6/6 pass): transaction creation updates budgets immediately, all pages are reachable via navigation, custom categories appear consistently across all features, empty states show helpful messages, split transactions correctly distribute spending to budgets, and month boundaries are correctly handled without date confusion.
